title: Nursery Rhyme. CXL. Songs.
content:
About the bush, Willy,
            About the bee-hive,
        About the bush, Willy,
            I'll meet thee alive.

        Then to my ten shillings,
            Add you but a groat,
        I'll go to Newcastle,
            And buy a new coat.

        Five and five shillings,
            Five and a crown;
        Five and five shillings,
            Will buy a new gown.

        Five and five shillings,
            Five and a groat;
        Five and five shillings,
            Will buy a new coat.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CXL. Songs." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
